message.content:
Explore /home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/gru/ thoroughly. I need to understand this example to optimize its performance on Andes AX45MPV VLEN=1024 FPGA.

Report back:
1. The GRU model structure: layer shapes, dimensions (input size, hidden size, timesteps/sequence length, batch size)
2. Which libnn functions are called (full function names)
3. For each called function, estimate its expected hotness based on the layer shapes (size of matmuls, activations)
4. Any existing Makefile or how it would be built (likely needs a new Makefile similar to /home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/Makefile)
5. Whether functions like riscv_nn_fc_s16_s16_s16 or similar fully connected variants are used, and their internal implementation status (already optimized for RVV 1.0 + ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD, or still using older paths)
6. Compare against cifar10 example for structural similarity

The main file is nds_nn_examples_gru.c. Medium thoroughness. Report in under 400 words with code snippets of key call sites.

content.text: I'll explore the GRU example thoroughly to understand its structure and performance characteristics.

content.content:
1	################################################################################
2	# CIFAR-10 CNN example — build and run on Andes QEMU / FPGA (AX45MPV, VLEN=1024)
3	#
4	#   make            — build cifar10.elf (QEMU/correctness)
5	#   make run        — run on QEMU (strace mode, captures semihosting output)
6	#   make perf       — build and run on FPGA, report total inst+cycle count
7	#   make perf-layers — build and run on FPGA, report per-layer inst+cycle breakdown
8	#   make clean      — remove build artifacts
9	################################################################################
10	
11	LIBNN_ROOT := ../../../..
12	TOOLCHAIN_BIN := /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin
13	CC := $(TOOLCHAIN_BIN)/riscv64-elf-gcc
14	
15	QEMU := /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
16	QEMU_CFG := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
17	VLEN := 1024
18	QEMU_TIMEOUT := 30
19	
20	BOARD_WRAPPER := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
21	export MYPC  ?= sw-boards.andestech.com
22	export MYPORT ?= 1115
23	
24	BUILD_DIR := build
25	ELF       := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10.elf
26	ELF_PERF  := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_perf.elf
27	ELF_LAYERS := $(BUILD_DIR)/cifar10_layers.elf
28	SRC_ORIG  := nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c
29	SRC_PATCHED := $(BUILD_DIR)/nds_nn_examples_cifar10_patched.c
30	
31	CFLAGS := -O3 -Wall -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections
32	CFLAGS += -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS
33	CFLAGS += -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d
34	CFLAGS += -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=$(VLEN)
35	CFLAGS += -DFAST_VERSION
36	
37	INCS := -I$(BUILD_DIR)/shim
38	INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Include
39	INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/internal
40	INCS += -I.
41	INCS += -I$(LIBNN_ROOT)/Examples/unit_func/include
42	
43	LIBS := $(LIBNN_ROOT)/libnn.a -lm
44	
45	# Shim headers: map old nds_nn_*.h names to current riscv_nn_*.h
46	SHIM_HEADERS := $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_, \
47		softmax.h activation.h pooling.h fully_connected.h convolution.h)
48	
49	# Extra flags shared by both perf builds
50	PERF_CFLAGS := -DPF_COUNTER -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP
51	
52	.PHONY: all run perf perf-layers clean
53	
54	all: $(ELF)
55	
56	$(ELF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
57		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
58		@echo "Built: $@"
59	
60	# Patch: add missing batch=1 argument to riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8 calls
61	$(SRC_PATCHED): $(SRC_ORIG) | $(BUILD_DIR)
62		sed \
63		  -e 's/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\(img_buffer[12]\), \(CONV[123]_OUT_DIM\)/riscv_nn_maxpool_HWC_s8(\1, 1, \2/g' \
64		  $< > $@
65	
66	# Generate shim headers
67	$(BUILD_DIR)/shim/nds_nn_%.h: | $(BUILD_DIR)/shim
68		echo '#include "riscv_nn_$*.h"' > $@
69	
70	$(BUILD_DIR)/shim:
71		mkdir -p $@
72	
73	$(BUILD_DIR):
74		mkdir -p $@
75	
76	run: $(ELF)
77		@echo "[run] QEMU VLEN=$(VLEN) (timeout=$(QEMU_TIMEOUT)s, strace mode)"
78		@STRACE_LOG=$(BUILD_DIR)/strace.log; \
79		strace -f -e trace=write -o $$STRACE_LOG \
80		  $(QEMU) -readconfig $(QEMU_CFG) -M andes_ae350 \
81		  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=$(VLEN) \
82		  -nographic -semihosting -bios $(ELF) > /dev/null 2>&1 & \
83		SPID=$$!; \
84		sleep $(QEMU_TIMEOUT); \
85		kill $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
86		wait $$SPID 2>/dev/null || true; \
87		echo "[output]"; \
88		grep -E 'write\((9|1),' $$STRACE_LOG \
89		  | sed "s/.*write([0-9]*, \"//; s/\\\\n\".*//; s/\".*//"
90	
91	$(ELF_PERF): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
92		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
93		@echo "Built: $@"
94	
95	$(ELF_LAYERS): $(SRC_PATCHED) $(SHIM_HEADERS) | $(BUILD_DIR)
96		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(PERF_CFLAGS) -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE $(INCS) -o $@ $(SRC_PATCHED) $(LIBS)
97		@echo "Built: $@"
98	
99	perf: $(ELF_PERF)
100		@echo "[perf] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
101		@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
102		PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_PERF)
103	
104	perf-layers: $(ELF_LAYERS)
105		@echo "[perf-layers] Running on FPGA board $(MYPC):$(MYPORT)"
106		@rm -f $(LIBNN_ROOT)/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
107		PATH=$(TOOLCHAIN_BIN):$$PATH $(BOARD_WRAPPER) $(ELF_LAYERS)
108	
109	clean:
110		rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)
111

18	 ******************************************************************************/
19	
20	/** @file*/
21	
22	#include "riscv_nn_activation.h"
23	#include "internal_nn_table.h"
24	#include "internal_nn_math.h"
25	
26	//// Activations Functions
27	
28	void riscv_nn_activate_s16(q15_t * in_out, uint32_t size, uint16_t int_bits, riscv_nn_activation_fun  act_fun)
29	{
30	#ifdef ENA_EMPTY_FUN
31	    //empty body
32	#else
33	    uint16_t  shift_size = 8 + 3 - int_bits;
34	    uint32_t  bit_mask = 0x7FF >> int_bits;
35	    uint32_t  full_frac = bit_mask + 1;
36	    const q15_t *lookup_table;
37	
38	    switch (act_fun)
39	    {
40	    case NN_SIGMOID:
41	        lookup_table = sigmoidTable_q15;
42	        break;
43	    case NN_TANH:
44	    default:
45	        lookup_table = tanhTable_q15;
46	        break;
47	    }
48	
49	#if defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) && defined(ENA_VEC_ISA_STRIDE_LS) && 0
50	    long vl;
51	    //index load instructions' performance is not good as vrgather; thus, we
52	    //should use the vrgather algorithm first
53	    while(size > 0)
54	    {
55	        NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(vl, size, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_SEW_E16, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_LMUL_M4);
56	        NDS_VEC_VLH_V(NDS_VEC_V0, in_out);
57	        NDS_VEC_VAND_VX(NDS_VEC_V4, NDS_VEC_V0, bit_mask);          //frac
58	        NDS_VEC_VSRA_VX(NDS_VEC_V8, NDS_VEC_V0, shift_size);        //index
59	        NDS_VEC_VADD_VI(NDS_VEC_V12, NDS_VEC_V8, 1);                //index2
60	        NDS_VEC_VAND_VX(NDS_VEC_V8, NDS_VEC_V8, 0xff);
61	        NDS_VEC_VAND_VX(NDS_VEC_V12, NDS_VEC_V12, 0xff);
62	        NDS_VEC_VSLL_VI(NDS_VEC_V8, NDS_VEC_V8, 1);                 //note. indices use BYTE as offset unit
63	        NDS_VEC_VSLL_VI(NDS_VEC_V12, NDS_VEC_V12, 1);               //note. indices use BYTE as offset unit
64	        NDS_VEC_VLXH_V(NDS_VEC_V8, lookup_table, NDS_VEC_V8);       //value
65	        NDS_VEC_VLXH_V(NDS_VEC_V12, lookup_table, NDS_VEC_V12);     //value2
66	
67	        NDS_VEC_VRSUB_VX(NDS_VEC_V20, NDS_VEC_V4, full_frac);       //full_frac - frac
68	        NDS_VEC_VWMUL_VV(NDS_VEC_V24, NDS_VEC_V20, NDS_VEC_V8);     //(full_frac - frac) * value
69	        NDS_VEC_VWMACC_VV(NDS_VEC_V24, NDS_VEC_V12, NDS_VEC_V4);    //(q31_t) (full_frac - frac) * value + (q31_t) value2 * frac)
70	        NDS_VEC_VNSRA_VX(NDS_VEC_V24, NDS_VEC_V24, shift_size);
71	        NDS_VEC_VSH_V(NDS_VEC_V24, in_out);
72	        in_out += vl;
73	        size -= vl;
74	    }
75	#elif defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) && (NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION == 80)
76	    long vl;
77	    //load lookup_table into registers
78	    NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(vl, 256, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_SEW_E16, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_LMUL_M8);  //lookup_table size is 256
79	    NDS_VEC_VLH_V(NDS_VEC_V0, lookup_table);    //v0~v7
80

content.text:
Now let me create a comprehensive summary based on my findings:

## GRU Example Analysis Report

### 1. GRU Model Structure

The GRU model in `nds_nn_examples_gru.c` has these dimensions:
- **Input size (DIM_INPUT):** 32
- **Hidden/history size (DIM_HISTORY):** 32
- **Vector dimension (DIM_VEC):** 64
- **Batch size:** 1 (implicit, operates on single samples)
- **Timesteps:** 2 iterations (processed sequentially in main loop)
- **Total scratch buffer:** DIM_HISTORY * 4 + DIM_INPUT = 160 q15_t (320 bytes)

Memory layout in scratch_input:
- reset gate: [0:32]
- input: [32:64]
- history: [64:96]
- update gate: [96:128]
- hidden_state: [128:160]

### 2. Called libnn Functions

**Key matrix-vector operations (hottest):**
- `riscv_nn_fc_mat_vec_s16_s16_s8_sft_bias_fast()` - 3x per iteration (reset, update, hidden)
- `riscv_nn_fc_mat_vec_s16_s16_s8_sft_bias()` - fallback variant (same 3x, controlled by USE_X4 flag)

**Activation/element-wise (all in-place):**
- `riscv_nn_activate_s16()` - 4x per iteration (sigmoid on reset/update, tanh on hidden_state, and one more sigmoid)
- `nds_mul_q15()` - 3x per iteration
- `nds_offset_q15()` - 1x per iteration
- `nds_sub_q15()` - 1x per iteration
- `nds_dup_q15()` - 2x (initialization only)

### 3. Hotness Estimation

**Rank by operation count (per GRU cell):**

1. **riscv_nn_fc_mat_vec_s16_s16_s8_sft_bias_fast()** - HOTTEST
   - 3 calls per iteration × (input_size + history_size) × history_size matmul
   - Each: 3 × (32+32) × 32 = 6,144 MACs per call × 3 calls = 18,432 MACs/iteration
   - With RVV1.0 intrinsics, should vectorize fully (vwmacc operations in code)

2. **riscv_nn_activate_s16()** - HOT (table lookups with interpolation)
   - 4 calls × 32 elements with sigmoid/tanh table lookups
   - Uses linear interpolation; RVV1.0 vectorized (vrgather + VWMACC pattern)

3. **nds_mul_q15()** - MODERATE (element-wise)
   - 3 × 32 q15 multiplies

### 4. Build Configuration

**No Makefile exists** in /home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/gru/ yet. Follow cifar10/Makefile pattern:

```makefile
LIBNN_ROOT := ../../../..
TOOLCHAIN_BIN := /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin
CC := $(TOOLCHAIN_BIN)/riscv64-elf-gcc
CFLAGS := -O3 -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d
CFLAGS += -DENA_VEC_ISA -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DMAX_VLEN=1024
CFLAGS += -DFAST_VERSION -DPF_COUNTER -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP
```

The GRU currently compiles but has **no build automation**. Requires new Makefile with shim headers for old nds_nn_*.h → riscv_nn_*.h mapping.

### 5. Function Implementation Status

**riscv_nn_fc_mat_vec_s16_s16_s8_sft_bias_fast():** Lines 39-48 show RVV 1.0 intrinsic path is ACTIVE:
```c
#elif defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) && (NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION >= 100) && defined(ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC)
    nn_dup_s16_x4_reordered_v2(in_vec, tmp_buf, size);  // input reordering for block matmul
    // Advanced mask generation for 4-row unrolling with vwmacc
```
- Uses vwmacc (widening multiply-accumulate) - exploits full vectorization
- Input reordering (nn_dup_s16_x4_reordered_v2) for 4-row batching
- **No ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD** guard found (dot product not used for this s16→s16→s8 path)

**riscv_nn_activate_s16():** Line 49+ shows RVV 1.0 table-based path optimized:
```c
#elif defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) && (NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION == 80)
```
Uses vrgather for table lookup + vwmacc for interpolation (no dot-prod needed).

### 6. Structural Comparison vs. CIFAR-10

| Aspect | GRU | CIFAR-10 |
|--------|-----|----------|
| Architecture | RNN/GRU cell | CNN (conv+pool+FC) |
| Hottest op | FC matmul (input+history)→hidden | Convolution (3×3 with im2col) |
| Batch size | 1 | 1 |
| Data types | q15→q8→q15 (s16 weights) | u8→s8→u8 (s8 weights) |
| Timesteps | 2 sequential | Single pass |
| Use of RVV | RVV 1.0 intrinsics (vwmacc) | RVV 1.0 intrinsics (vwmacc) |
| Perf counters | Yes (FUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE) | Yes (same PF_COUNTER macro) |

Both use identical RVV 1.0 intrinsic strategy; GRU has temporal loop instead of spatial.

File locations:
- Main code: /home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/gru/nds_nn_examples_gru.c
- FC implementations: /home/nick/work/libnn/Source/FullyConnectedFunctions/riscv_nn_fc_mat_vec_s16_s16_s8_sft_bias*.c
- Activation: /home/nick/work/libnn/Source/ActivationFunctions/riscv_nn_activate_s16.c
- Reference Makefile: /home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples/cifar10/Makefile
